inherited_task_a_carrier.expected_implementation_scope: Controller-only request-order and admission changes plus focused tests and regenerated bindings; no server-engine change is expected.
inherited_task_a_carrier.expected_scientific_value: Directly tests whether the already-generated Task-A execution can serve as the carrier and turn demonstrated suffix reuse into strict complete marginal fresh-token advantage after measured closure.
inherited_task_a_carrier.minimum_change: Issue catalytic Task B immediately after captured Task A on the same cache-enabled slot and omit the separate fresh zero-output checkpoint materialization.
inherited_task_a_carrier.required_live_gate: Each catalytic Task-B request must reuse at least the exact statically reconstructed post-Task-A terminal prefix, with runtime cached-token evidence bound before scoring; failure is terminally inconclusive and is not repaired by rematerialization.

pure_closure_or_root_copy.expected_scientific_value: An exact snapshot/root mechanism could establish restoration independently, but closure alone cannot establish strict one-branch fresh-token advantage under the observed arithmetic.
reasons: Perfect closure alone would only remove the observed 528-token disadvantage and return the prior single-branch route to equality with direct replay.
The current sidecar launch does not enable the slot save/restore HTTP surface, and internal sequence-copy support is not exposed as a frozen root-copy controller path.
A durable branch tree is a later architecture boundary, not the minimum current successor.

two_branch_amortization.expected_implementation_scope: Extend each matched pair to two frozen related Task-B branches with equivalent-information fresh direct replay and one measured closure after each catalytic branch.
two_branch_amortization.expected_scientific_value: Tests whether one materialized checkpoint can amortize its cost across two useful downstream transformations when inherited continuation is unavailable.
two_branch_amortization.projection_law: For two matched Task-B branches, direct minus catalytic equals one checkpoint materialization minus two observed closure costs: 4185 - 528 - 528 = 3129.
two_branch_amortization.reason: It adds a second downstream branch and more requests before testing the simpler already-resident Task-A carrier path.

minimum_successor_requirements.carrier_geometry: One process-local slot carries the just-completed Task-A terminal state directly into one related Task-B continuation.
minimum_successor_requirements.closure: Retain one immediate zero-output closure/readdress measurement after catalytic Task B; report exact and partial closure separately and do not require exact closure for request continuation.
minimum_successor_requirements.direct_baseline: Run the same-information direct Task-B route with cache_prompt=false after closure so it cannot supply or preserve the inherited carrier.
minimum_successor_requirements.protected_scoring: Keep protected answers unopened until all 12 captures exist, cleanup completes, and postflight passes; publish only boolean correctness and aggregates.
minimum_successor_requirements.scientific_decision_law: Support inherited-carrier fresh-token advantage only if all four pairs complete, catalytic Task-B accuracy is at least direct accuracy, every catalytic request passes the exact inherited-prefix reuse gate, and catalytic versus direct fresh-token-per-correct cross-products are strictly lower after including closure.
minimum_successor_requirements.task_a_reporting: Report Task A once and separately; preserve answer accuracy and textual latent-state accuracy as separate facts, but do not make the prior 3/4 textual latent-state score an admission gate for executable carrier reuse.
minimum_successor_requirements.task_b_fairness: Freeze held-out Task-B questions and protected answers before contact, preserve all four pairs, give both routes the same captured Task-A JSON and semantic evidence, and use the same Task-B seed within each pair.

selection_reason: The current one-slot process-local runtime already retains the Task-A terminal state and supports an immediate cache-enabled same-slot continuation. Removing only the redundant fresh materialization tests the smallest causal change implied by neo-exp-0048 before adding another branch or a new root-copy architecture.

source_feasibility_evidence.finding: The current controller runs Task A, then performs a separate cache-disabled zero-output materialization before catalytic Task B.
The live sidecar is one-slot and cache-enabled, and the request helper targets slot zero with cache_prompt=true.

The server selects an exact requested slot, computes common-prefix reuse when cache_prompt is true, retains accepted generated tokens in slot state, and clears prior prompt state when cache_prompt is false.

static_limitations.note: Existing captures support the geometry but do not preserve native generated-token arrays sufficient to prove the future Task-B request's exact inherited common prefix. That identity must be reconstructed before contact and observed at runtime.
static_limitations.route_order_risk: With one slot, a cache-disabled direct request would destroy the inherited state if run first. Fixed inherited-before-direct order is therefore structural and must be disclosed.
